testSteps.description: Given I have test aggregate properties with id "test-aggregate-id", name "Test Aggregate", and version 1
And I have test passport with userId "test-user-id" and permissions ["read", "write"]
Given I have created a test aggregate
When I add 100 domain events rapidly
And I add 100 integration events rapidly
Then the aggregate should have 100 domain events

children.description: Test Actor ensures that 100 does equal 100

testSteps.description: And the aggregate should have 100 integration events

children.description: Test Actor ensures that 100 does equal 100

testSteps.description: When I clear all domain events
And I clear all integration events
Then the aggregate should have no domain events

children.description: Test Actor ensures that 0 does equal 0

testSteps.description: And the aggregate should have no integration events

children.description: Test Actor ensures that 0 does equal 0

userStory.narrative:
As a domain developer
I want to use aggregate roots to manage domain and integration events
So that I can build robust domain models with event sourcing capabilities

actors.name: Test Actor
can: ScheduleWork { scheduler: { clock: { timeAdjustment: { milliseconds: 0 } }, interactionTimeout: { milliseconds: 5000 } } }
